steps.title: While holding with wide-tip featherweight forceps,...
steps.instruction: While holding with wide-tip featherweight forceps, rinse patent Biomphalaria glabrata snails, individually, with MilliQ water - Rinsing snails individually prevents cross-contamination of cercariae from another snail - Snails should be at least 5 weeks post infection with a single miracidium

steps.title: Place rinsed snails into individual wells...
steps.instruction: Place rinsed snails into individual wells of 6- or 12-well plates

steps.title: Add 3-4ml of MilliQ water to each well
steps.instruction: Add 3-4ml of MilliQ water to each well

steps.title: Induce shedding of cercariae by place...
steps.instruction: Induce shedding of cercariae by place snails under a direct light for up to 2 hours or until cercariae are visible in well with a naked eye

steps.title: After 2 hrs, remove snails that...
steps.instruction: After 2 hrs, remove snails that are not shedding cercariae and place into a new tank with food. These snails can be re-shed in one week. Some monomiracidium-infected snails will not begin shedding until up to 8 weeks after exposure. At this time, if they are still not shedding cercariae, it is safe to assume they were not infected and can be disposed of properly.

steps.title: For snails that are shedding low...
steps.instruction: For snails that are shedding low amounts of cercariae (<100), place into a new tank with food and re-shed in one week

steps.title: Put an "X" on the wells...
steps.instruction: Put an "X" on the wells of the snails that are shedding and uniquely number each. This numbering must be preserved throughout.

steps.title: Prepare numbered 5ml Eppendorf tubes each...
steps.instruction: Prepare numbered 5ml Eppendorf tubes each fitted with a 100Âṁm pluriStrainer Mini

steps.title: Using a new sterile transfer pipet...
steps.instruction: Using a new sterile transfer pipet for each snail, pipette water containing the cercariae through the pluriStrainer

steps.title: Rinse the well and snails with...
steps.instruction: Rinse the well and snails with fresh MilliQ water and collect this water as above to fill each 5ml tube to the top If you do not have time to complete the DNA isolation, PCR, and gel electrophoresis in a single day, snails can be kept in 6-well plates with a fresh water change daily. Be sure to maintain the numbering of the snails.

steps.title: Discard pluriStrainers, close, and submerge 5ml...
steps.instruction: Discard pluriStrainers, close, and submerge 5ml tubes containing cercariae into ice for 00:30:00

steps.title: Centrifuge tubes at 2000 rpm, 4 ÂḞC, 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Centrifuge tubes at 2000 rpm, 4 ÂḞC, 30 minutes

steps.title: After centrifugation, place tubes immediately back...
steps.instruction: After centrifugation, place tubes immediately back on ice and aspirate/pipette off liquid, taking care not to disrupt the cercariae pellet. Try to leave no more than 50Âṁl of water on the cercariae pellet as the more dilute the lysate is, the more difficult amplification will be.

steps.title: Add 1x volume of 2x Alkaline...
steps.instruction: Add 1x volume of 2x Alkaline Lysis Reagent to each cercariae pellet and mix well (i.e. add 25Âṁl 2x Alkaline Lysis Reagent to 25Âṁl of cercariae in MilliQ) 2x Alkaline Lysis Reagent 25ml H 2 O 125Âṁl 10N NaOH 20Âṁl 0.5M disodium EDTA Make fresh every 1-2 months Store at Room temperature

steps.title: Incubate at 95 ÂḞC for ~1...
steps.instruction: Incubate at 95 ÂḞC for ~1 hr or until cercariae dissolve, mixing at least every 15 min. Centrifuge using a mini-centrifuge if needed

steps.title: After tissue is dissolved, place on ice to cool
steps.instruction: After tissue is dissolved, place on ice to cool

steps.title: Add 1x volume of 2x Neutralizing...
steps.instruction: Add 1x volume of 2x Neutralizing Reagent and briefly vortex to mix (i.e. add 50Âṁl 2x Neutralizing Reagent to 50Âṁl of lysate) 1M Tris-HCl 6.057g Tris-HCl To 50ml with nuclease-free H 2 O Store at Room temperature 2x Neutralizing Reagent 23ml H 2 O 2ml 1M Tris-HCl Store at Room temperature

steps.title: Run the PCR program as follows for both primer sets
steps.instruction: Run the PCR program as follows for both primer sets: %s %s Initial denaturation94ÂḞC2 minDenature98ÂḞC5 secx25Anneal and extend60ÂḞC15 secHold4ÂḞC~

steps.title: Prepare two PCR master mixes on...
steps.instruction: Prepare two PCR master mixes on ice (for actin control and W1 primer sets) each with enough for your samples, 1 female positive control, 1 male negative control, and 1 water blank control (i.e. if you have 8 samples, you will need 11 reactions for actin primers and 11 reactions for W primers) W1a - 5â CAA CAC AGT GAA ATT CTT CC 3â W1b - 5â GAA TTC ACC ACT CGA CAT TC 3â SmAct-F - 5â CAG TGT TCC CTT CCA TCG TT 3â SmAct-R - 5â GGA CAG GGT GTT CTT CTG GA 3â Mock-up of layout for 8 samples and their controls in four 8-well PCR strips A B C 1 Reagent Final concentration Volume per reaction 2 Nuclease-free water - 6.32Âṁl 3 Platinum II Taq 0.04U/Âṁl 0.08Âṁl 4 5x Platinum II Buffer 1x 2Âṁl 5 10mM dNTP 0.2mM each 0.2Âṁl 6 10uM F 0.2uM 0.2Âṁl 7 10uM R 0.2uM 0.2Âṁl 8 DNA* - 1Âṁl 9 10Âṁl reaction *If you think your DNA concentration is really low/high, you can measure first on Nanodrop (blank with Neutralization Buffer) and change input volume as necessary. Keep in mind that using more than 10% of the overall volume may inhibit the PCR reaction.

steps.title: Run a 1% agarose gel to...
steps.instruction: Run a 1% agarose gel to analyze 5-10Âṁl of your PCR product. Males will have PCR product band for actin only; Females will have PCR product band for actin and W1.
